title: Residential Support Worker
description:
This is a temporary, ad-hoc role with flexible shifts, including earlies, lates, sleep-ins, and waking nights.

You will support young people in the care system, helping them build independence and resilience while managing complex needs and risks.

Key Details: Pay: £14.82 with uplifts for wake nights and weekends.

Location: Cranmer Bank, Leeds.

Key Responsibilities: Provide person-centred care and emotional support to young people, manage ligature risks, assist with routines and personal care, maintain records and risk assessments, and respond to incidents in line with safeguarding procedures.

Essential Requirements: At least 1 year of experience in a similar setting, strong safeguarding awareness, and ligature training.

Desirable: Experience in Mental Health Secure settings.
summary:
Flexible residential support role in Leeds supporting young people in care.
Temporary shifts with safeguarding, routines and resilience-building at the core.

title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
This role supports young people aged 12-17 in mainstream residential homes, helping create stability, promote independence and maintain a safe, nurturing environment.

It is ad-hoc temporary work, suited to people who want flexibility while still making a meaningful difference.

Key Details: Various locations in Leeds, £14.82 per hour plus uplifts for waking nights and weekends, with earlies, lates, sleep-ins and waking nights available.

Requirements: Ligature training, at least 1 year of relevant experience in residential childcare or youth support, a strong grasp of safeguarding and de-escalation, and confidence supporting young people who may present challenging behaviour.

Desirable: Mental Health Secure experience or additional behavioural management training.
summary:
Flexible youth residential support role in Leeds for work with children in care.
Temporary shifts focused on safety, stability and positive day-to-day support.

title: Residential Childcare Support Worker
description:
Shift pattern is ad-hoc cover on a sessional or casual basis.

This Leeds role is for committed and enthusiastic support staff covering waking night residential childcare shifts as needed across the business.

Salary: £12.29 per hour.
summary:
Casual residential childcare support role in Leeds with waking night cover.
Ad-hoc shifts suited to flexible support staff.

title: Assistant Residential Support Worker
description:
This is a single-site adult residential support role on a rota-based 4-week schedule, starting at £13.50 per hour.

You will support adults with mental health challenges, learning disabilities and other complex needs, helping them live as independently and safely as possible. It is a non-personal care role, with the focus on relationships, daily living support and positive outcomes.

Key responsibilities include supporting routines, encouraging community involvement, monitoring wellbeing, maintaining records, and working with colleagues and external professionals.

Requirements include relevant adult residential care experience, a full UK driving licence with access to a vehicle, rota flexibility, and an enhanced DBS with Adults Barred List check.

Desirable: Level 3 Health and Social Care or willingness to work towards it, plus knowledge of mental health legislation or therapeutic approaches.
summary:
Residential support role in Halifax working with adults with mental health or learning disability needs.
Single-site rota role focused on safe daily living support and consistency.

title: Complex Care Assistant
description:
This Pudsey complex care role pays £15.00 per hour including holiday pay.

A permanent contract is available after successful probation.

Applicants must have the right to work in the UK, be a driver with access to their own car, and meet the role’s specific client-care requirements. The post is open to female applicants only under the Equality Act 2010 due to those care needs.
summary:
Complex care role in Pudsey for carers able to support specific client needs.
Driver required, with hourly pay and potential permanent progression after probation.

title: Care Assistant
description:
This Wakefield care role focuses on practical help, companionship and supporting clients to stay independent in their own homes.

You will provide personalised home care that helps people feel safe, supported and able to maintain a better quality of life.

The role offers full training, ongoing support and development, and is suited to people with a caring, reliable and positive attitude. No prior experience is required if you are willing to complete essential training, including First Aid and DBS checks.

Please note that sponsorship is not available for this role.
summary:
Community care role in Wakefield delivering practical help and companionship to clients.
Permanent position with a clear salary range and home-based care work.
